name: L-Carvone
description: L-Carvone — косметический ингредиент.
note: Low penalty in all categories.

name: Lactic Acid
description: Lactic acid is a fruit acid (AHA). It is used in cosmetics as a PH regulator, helps soften and retain moisture in the skin, and give shine. It promotes the hydration of the skin and the evacuation of dead cells. Lactic acid is produced through a fermentation process using specific bacteria such as Lactobacillus. It is naturally present in milk, wine, some vegetables and fruits. It is authorized in organic.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: L-Limonene
description: L-limonene is one of two isometric forms of limonene. It is found naturally in pine and mint oils. It is a regulated allergen in Europe.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Lac
description: Lac — косметический ингредиент.

name: L-Menthol
description: Naturally present in mint oil (Mentha arvensis, Mentha piperita), L-menthol is used to reconstitute mint scents in cosmetics. Among menthol isomers, L-menthol is the only one to provoke a sensation of cold. It is a regulated allergen in Europe.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Lanolin
description: Lanolin, also called wool fat or wool wax, is obtained by purification and refining of the suint (fat part of the wool). Lanolin is used to moisturize skin, hair and nails. It acts as a lubricant of the skin, which gives it a soft and smooth appearance. It helps to form emulsions and blends well with almost any other substance used in cosmetics and personal care products. Lanolin also has adhesive characteristics.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Animal.

name: Laureth-10
description: Laureth-10 is a very mild nonionic surfactant. It is produced by ethoxylation from lauric alcohol. The number 10 indicates the average number of ethylene oxide units repeated in the molecule. It is often used in cosmetics as an emulsifying agent (allows fatty substances to mix easily with water).
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Laureth-23
description: Laureth-23 is a nonionic surfactant prepared from lauryl alcohol and 23 moles of ethylene oxide: the number 23 therefore represents the average number of ethylene oxide molecules repeated in the molecule. It is used in cosmetics as an emulsifier and surfactant.It is used in cosmetics as a surfactant and emulsifier: HLB (16.9). It can be used to create oil-in-water emulsions.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

description: Lauryl glucoside is a mild nonionic surfactant of plant origin. It is formed from palm / coconut oil and sugar. It produces little foam but can thicken solutions, it is also used to control if necessary the pH value. It is authorized in organic cosmetics.
common_product_types: 2-in-1 shampoo and body wash for baby (23.4%)

name: Lavandula Angustifolia Oil
description: The essential oil of true lavender or fine lavender is highly concentrated in Linalool (it contains between 30 and 40%). It is attributed many analgesic, calming and even healing properties. It calms muscle excitement (cramps ..), regulates the nervous system or even promotes sleep. It is one of the most used essential oils in cosmetics.It contains the following allergens:- Linalool (very high concentration> 25%);- Geraniol (<1.5%)- Limonene (<2%)- Coumarin (<0.5%)
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Vegetal.

It gets along particularly well with our skin to promote its hydration and elasticity. It is used as an emulsifier to create oil-in-water emulsions or vice versa. It is allowed in organic.

name: Limonene
description: Limonene is one of Europe's regulated allergens. It takes its name from the lemon in which it is present in significant quantities. In cosmetics, it is used for its fragrance.It exists in two different forms:- d-limonene is present in many plants, fruits and vegetables. It is also found in citrus peel, gherkins, celery and many essential oils.- the other isometric form, l-limonene, is found mainly in pine oils, turpentine and mint oils.
